title: BIOL 105 - Principles of Biology
description: Fundamental processes of life. Structure and function of cells, genetics, evolution, and ecology.

title: CHEM 109 - General Chemistry I
description: Fundamental concepts: chemical foundations, stoichiometry, chemical reactions, gases, thermochemistry, atomic theory, bonding, liquids, solutions. For students in science, engineering, and related majors.

title: CHEM 110 - General Chemistry II
description: Fundamental concepts: kinetics; equilibrium; acids and bases; acid-base, solubility, and complex ion equilibria; entropy and free energy; electrochemistry; qualitative analysis. For students in science, engineering, and related majors.

title: ENGR 115 - Introduction to Engineering
description: Case studies in water quality, water resources, energy resources, and mechanical systems.

title: ENGR 205 - Introduction to Design
description: Engineering design process through real client-based projects iterating from opportunity identification, empathy building and research through to creative problem solving, analysis and testing. Special emphasis on teamwork, time management, and communication that includes public speaking to a range of audiences. Introduction to spreadsheets, word documents, and computer-aided design.

title: ENGR 210 - Solid Mechanics: Statics
description: Particle and rigid body equilibrium; vector concepts; equivalent systems of forces; centroids; moments of inertia; friction.

title: ENGR 225 - Computational Methods for Engineering I
description: Introduction to computer computational methods for engineering analysis and design using Python and other programming languages.

title: ENGR 226 - Computational Methods for Engineering II
description: Introduction to numerical methods for engineering analysis, design and resource management using Python and other programming languages.

title: MATH 109 - Calculus I
description: Limits, continuity, derivatives, integrals, and their applications.

title: MATH 110 - Calculus II
description: Logarithmic and exponential functions, inverse trigonometric functions, techniques of integration, infinite sequences and series, conic sections, polar coordinates.

title: MATH 210 - Calculus III
description: Vectors; parametric equations; 3-dimensional analytic geometry; vector- valued functions; partial derivatives; multiple integrals; introduction to line integrals.

title: PHYX 109 - General Physics A: Mechanics
description: Calculus-based, for science/engineering students.

title: PHYX 211 - General Physics C: Electricity, Magnetism
description: Calculus-based, for science/ engineering students.

title: ENGR 112 - First-Year Seminar Engineering
description: Introduce students to functions and resources of the university and support a successful transition to engineering and college. Transition curriculum in this course will integrate guest lectures, discussion, student reflection projects, computer labs, and help sessions. It is strongly recommended, although not required, that this course be taken concurrently with ENGR 115.

title: ENGR 123 - Engineering Fabrication
description: Safety procedures and the use of machine shop tools including bench, layout, precision measurement, power saws, drill presses, rotary tables, indexing devices, lathe, mill cutting, and hand tools.

title: ENGR 280 - Selected Topics in Engineering
description: Selected topics offered at the lower division level as demand warrants.

title: ENGR 313 - Systems Analysis
description: Microeconomics, systems analysis, and math modeling, linear and nonlinear optimization. Case studies in resource management. Engineering design applications.

title: ENGR 322 - Risk and Data Analysis for Engineers
description: Introduction to probability theory, probabilistic models, and stochastic processes. Parameter estimation and model evaluation for environmental and technological systems models with applications in engineering.

title: ENGR 326 - Computational Methods for Engineering III
description: Analytical and numerical methods for ordinary and partial differential equations used in engineering analysis and design.

title: ENGR 331 - Thermodynamics and Energy Systems I
description: Thermodynamics’ 1st and 2nd laws; thermodynamic properties of materials; thermodynamic processes; system and control volume analysis; application to energy systems.

title: ENGR 333 - Fluid Mechanics
description: Fluid properties; fluid statics; flow concepts; control volume analysis; continuity; energy and momentum concepts; boundary layer concepts; drag theory, flow measurements; flow in pipes/ducts; open channel flow; dimensional analysis and similitude. Engineering design applications.

title: ENGR 355 - Energy Systems Engineering Fundamentals
description: Apply thermodynamics and systems analysis to contemporary energy systems topics in their social, economic, and environmental contexts. Sustainable energy resources, buildings, transportation, and infrastructure.

title: ENGR 411 - Energy Project Development and Policy
description: Develop implementation plans for energy projects and analyze the effects of energy policy. Energy project planning, timelines, financial analysis, impact assessment, and stakeholder engagement. Energy policy institutions, mechanisms, and markets.

title: ENGR 417 - Heat and Mass Transport Processes
description: Theory of heat and mass transport and applications in energy and mechanical systems. Conduction, convection, radiation, diffusion, reaction kinetics, ideal reactor models.

title: ENGR 492W - Capstone Design Project
description: Culminating engineering design experience based on knowledge gained from previous coursework. Application of the engineering design process to develop a system, process, or management plan to solve a significant, open-ended engineering problem. To be taken final senior semester (within 16 units of graduation).

title: PHYX 315 - Introduction to Electronics and Electronic Instrumentation
description: Devices and circuits, both analog and digital, in science instrumentation. Construct amplifiers and digital circuits.

title: ENGR 471 - Thermodynamics and Energy Systems II
description: Continues ENGR 331. Applications of 2nd law of thermodynamics. Irreversibility, availability, power and refrigeration cycles, combustion, and phase equilibria. Engineering design applications.

title: ENGR 473 - Building Energy Analysis
description:
Thermodynamics applied to energy analysis of buildings. Heating and ventilating systems; lighting; building envelopes; process loads. Analyze campus buildings. Engineering design applications.
Co-listed with: ENGR 573. (Only one of the co-listed courses may be taken for credit. See Terminology for more information.)

title: ENGR 475 - Renewable Energy Power Systems
description:
Principles of hydro, wind, and photovoltaic power production and systems. Engineering design applications.
Co-listed with: ENGR 575. (Only one of the co-listed courses may be taken for credit. See Terminology for more information.)

title: ENGR 478 - Electricity Grids and Distributed Renewable Energy
description:
Foundations and topics in the design and operation of electric power systems (“the Grid”), integrating renewable electricity generation with the grid, and distributed energy systems with generation, storage, and demand-side management.
Co-listed with: ENGR 578 (Only one of the co-listed courses may be taken for credit. See Terminology for more information.)

title: ESM 305 - Environmental Conflict Resolution
description: Introduction to conflict theory as applied in complex natural resource disputes. Skill development in planning culturally appropriate and inclusive public participation processes, meeting facilitation, and conflict mediation. Comparison of options for nonviolent conflict management.

title: ESM 325 - Environmental Law and Regulation
description: Overview of laws, policy, and institutions used to regulate natural resource management and protect the environment. Legal principles; property rights; federal, state, and international environmental legislation; and regulatory authorities.

title: ENST 295 - Power, Privilege and the Environment
description: Explores the environment as a central element in the reproduction of patterns of power and privilege along lines of race, class, and gender. Examines how environmental conflicts challenge those patterns.

title: ENST 480 - Special Topics
description: Special topics in environmental studies.

title: ENGR 305 - Appropriate Technology
description: Engineering technology principles. Energy, waste disposal, food production technologies. Lab exercises involve working systems at Campus Center for Appropriate Technology.

title: NAS 309 - Traditional Ecological Knowledge and Indigenous Natural Resource Management
description: Emphasis on ethical engagement of Traditional Ecological Knowledge; best practices for Indigenous natural resource management and the critical importance of Indigenous sciences and knowledges to addressing and developing natural resource management plans and projects. Comparison of the cultural traditions and beliefs of indigenous people, with those of western science based management.

title: NAS 332 - Environmental Justice
description: Issues/concerns that led to Executive Order 12898 (environmental policies and conflicts between industries and those seeking environmental protection, including Alaska Native villages, “lower 48” tribes, grassroot community organizations).

title: NAS 364 - Federal Indian Law I
description: Unique federal/tribal legal and historical relationship. Scope and authority of tribal governments as modified through contact with the federal government. Federal legislation and Supreme Court decisions regarding Indians and tribes.

title: CHEM 370 - Earth System Chemistry
description: Chemistry of the earth, including elemental cycling and speciation in the environment, the impact of man on biogeochemical processes, and the effects of climate change on the chemical/physical interactions occurring within and between the atmosphere, hydrosphere, and biosphere.

title: GEOL 303 - Earth Resources and Global Environmental Change
description: Origins, occurrence, and limits of important energy, mineral, and water resources. Societal and environmental impacts of resource use and global climate change. Cannot count for geology majors as upper division geology area of specialization.

title: OCN 420 - Oceans and Climate
description: Examines the role that oceans play in mediating global climate. Detailed exploration of ocean carbon cycle, consequences of climate change on ocean ecosystems, ocean-related climate feedback loops, and predictions of oceans of the future.
